item.title: A few ideas on code review
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
AfôÙter watchôÙing a talk about code reôÙview [1], from the last EuôÙroPyôÙthon, I got ôÙsome ideas that I'd like to sumôÙmarise.

Read moreãÎ (2 min remaining to read)

item.title: Upcoming talk at EuroPython 2016
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
I am glad to inôÙform that I will be speakôÙing at EuôÙroPyôÙthon 2016 conôÙferôÙence.

I'll speak @euôÙropyôÙthon 2016:
'Clean code in Python' httpôÙs://t.ôÙco/e4ôÙclWU3aDM

ã MarôÙiôÙano (@rôÙmarôÙiôÙanoa) March 27, 2016

My subôÙmisôÙsion about clean code in Python was acôÙceptôÙed, so in the next ediôÙtion of EuôÙroPyôÙthon 2016, in BilôÙbao, Spain, I will talk about clean code prinôÙciôÙples for Python. Here is the abôÙstracôÙt:

https://ep2016.europython.eu/conference/talks/clean-code-in-python

The full list of talks is availôÙable at:

https://ep2016.europython.eu/en/events/sessions/

If you are inôÙterôÙestôÙed, subôÙscribe to the EuôÙroPyôÙthon blog and Youtube chanôÙnel. I will inôÙclude more deôÙtailôÙs in a sepôÙaôÙrate post.

item.title: Deleting commented out code
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
This is a rule I alôÙways enôÙcourôÙage in softôÙware deôÙvelôÙopôÙmenôÙt. MoreôÙover, I conôÙsidôÙer it to be someôÙthing that has ôÙto be inôÙcludôÙed in evôÙery codôÙing guideôÙline of a good projecôÙt.

There are sevôÙerôÙal reaôÙsons for this, but probôÙaôÙbly the best exôÙplaôÙnaôÙtion can be found in the book "Clean Code"[1] by unôÙcle Bob, on which exôÙplains that the code, gets outôÙdatôÙed (rotôÙten) with the rest of the surôÙroundôÙing code, and hence it makes a place for conôÙfuôÙsion, leadôÙing to an erôÙror-prone spot.

There are, howôÙevôÙer, peoôÙple that seem to find some arôÙguôÙments for comôÙmentôÙing out code, or leavôÙing it. ôÙSome comôÙmon arôÙguôÙments/reaôÙsons usuôÙalôÙly are:

  • "I might need this funcôÙtionôÙalôÙiôÙty latôÙer.."

We have source conôÙtrol sysôÙtems (for exôÙamôÙple git) for this. In git, anyôÙthing can be reôÙstored from a preôÙviôÙous point. If the softôÙware is propôÙerôÙly unôÙder verôÙsion conôÙtrol, there is no reaôÙson to fear daôÙta loss. Trust git, code fearôÙlessôÙly.

  • "This is temôÙpoôÙrary disôÙabled... It will be reôÙstored latôÙer".

Again, same prinôÙciôÙple, reôÙly on the verôÙsion conôÙtrol sysôÙtem. Save a patch, and then reôÙstore latôÙer, or stash the changes, reôÙvert the comôÙmit, etc. As you see, there are plenôÙty of betôÙter opôÙtions for solvôÙing this sceôÙnariôÙo.

  • Code that was left from the fist verôÙsion

ProbôÙaôÙbly deôÙbugôÙging leftôÙoverôÙs. No doubt here: seek, loôÙcate, deôÙstroy.

There is, a clear probôÙlem with code that is unôÙder comôÙmenôÙt, which is that it is "frozen" in time: it was good at some point, but then it was left there while the rest of the code around it, evolved, so this old code might not cerôÙtainôÙly work (hence it is "rotôÙten"), so un-ôÙcomôÙmentôÙing it is a bad idea beôÙcause it will probôÙaôÙbly crash.

AnôÙothôÙer probôÙlem is that it can be a source of bias for some othôÙer deôÙvelôÙopôÙer, who wants to mainôÙtain that code at a fuôÙture point in time. The one who left the rotôÙten code, might have thought that it was a source of inôÙspiôÙraôÙtion for when this funcôÙtionôÙalôÙiôÙty was ôÙgoôÙing to be apôÙplied, but inôÙstead, it is just biôÙasôÙing the new deôÙvelôÙopôÙer with this skeleôÙton, preôÙventôÙing from a brand new, fresh idea for that code.

ThereôÙfore, for these main reaôÙsons (an probôÙaôÙbly much more), havôÙing code that is comôÙmentôÙed in a code base, is a poor pracôÙtice, (not to ôÙmenôÙtion a code smell). If you are a seaôÙsoned deôÙvelôÙopôÙer, who cares about code qualôÙiôÙty, and best pracôÙtices, you must not doubt when deletôÙing it. Delete comôÙmentôÙed out code merôÙciôÙlessôÙly: seek, loôÙcate and deôÙstroy.

[1]A book I highly recommend: https://www.amazon.com/Clean-Code-Handbook-Software-Craftsmanship/dp/0132350882
